Okay, welcome back. So in this lesson, we are going to take a deep dive through these seven elements within the Gestalt. We're going to talk about how to presence to them, recognize them, and really lead and coach that transformation back to the positive polarity. So just to reorient you, we are still here in part one unpacking the Gestalt, and we are going to be transforming a sense of longing to healthy desires, a sense of anxiety and urgency into positive anticipation, doubt to renewed possibility, failure to renewed confidence, confusion to clarity around what must be done, resistance into readiness to pursue that outcome, and a sense of wrongness to wrong path into rightness like this is it, this is my move, this is my move. Now so these are all the elements we are transforming. As we talked about throughout these negative elements of the pole tend to exist more in the past, and they tend to have more evidence to them, whereas these elements of the positive side, the positive polarity tend to be more present or future focused, right, and they require that transformation from turning all that experience from the past into a positive outcome. So that is the alchemy that is the transformation that we are seeking to gift our pre-clients with. Let's break down all these elements more deeply so you know how to recognize them and coach through them. So the first element is desire and the negative pole within desire, there will be a sense of longing, a sense of lack, a sense of pain and struggle and envy of others who have cracked that code, so to speak, injustice around how that's unfair, and even a sense of disconnection and vagueness to what that desire actually is if they haven't actually given themselves the gift of claiming it and owning that yes, this is what I want. So to be clear within desire and their goals for a certain outcome, it's going to include the painful longing for it, the envy of others who already have it, and the sense of unfairness that this might bring up. So when someone shares what they want, when someone on your call starts stating this is why I'm here, this is what I want, use your presence to attune to how it's being expressed. Remember that context is often more important than the content itself, so feel into it, does it feel light, does it feel empowered, or does it feel heavy, dense as if there is this whole anchoring aspect to it, this whole baggage, this whole density being carried along within that desire, and if so, you're going to have to coach it. But once again, if it's light, if it's empowered, if it's clear, then yeah, you might not actually have to weave through all those elements of a negative charge. So desire includes longing. Longing is a state of defeat of not having, right? Inherent within longing is a sense of I can't have this, right? This is not what I have. So longing is often included in desire. It gives them a sense of that goal, that outcome of being distant, out of reach, or even impossible. Desire also tends to include the fear of disappointing ourselves and or others if not attained, right? We tend to disown our desires and even downplay their importance to protect ourselves from the shame and not fulfilling them, right? A lot of us fear not attaining our desires more than we do staying stuck in our current situation, right? The fear of failure, the shame of failure is often heavier, more fearful, more resistance inducing than just staying put where we are. Meaning, even if you're showing up totally in service, many prospects will be disempowering and disqualifying themselves from what they want as a tend or attempt to preempt the possible shame and self disappointment in not attaining their desires. So this is really important to be aware of. This is what they might be coming to the call loaded with. So your goal is going to coach them in owning their desire, not just as a vague idea, not just as something abstract, but as a specific situational outcome that they can feel empowered to claim to implicate themselves within, right? So here are some questions that you may use as you coach them in claiming and stepping into healthy positive desire. You could ask, does it feel safe to declare that? How do you feel in your desire for a sustainable, I mean, that's right in career, right? If you knew you can fail, would you allow yourself to do this? Do you actually claim this desire? When you tune into that desire, is it just an idea or an inevitable outcome you're willing to do anything to achieve, right? This is moving it from the abstract to something they're implicated in. Do you feel any negative self talk or resistance that comes up when you state, I want X? You know, usually that's a great litmus test. Have your client have your precline say, I want this goal, right? And then they get flooded with all the reasons why that's impossible, all the reasons why they might not deserve it, all the fear in pursuing it and failing, right? That's a great way to extract and coax out some of those hidden voices or hidden saboteurs. When you look at others who have achieved this, do you think there's anything particularly special about them that you can't cultivate in yourself, right? So this might help them pierce through that sense of envy, that sense of unfairness and really give you an opportunity to coach them through those elements. Vague, non-dimensionalized desires are often the clearest sign of them disowning it and need to be transformed into dimensionalized situations, right? So someone might in their head believe I want to have a successful freelance career, but they haven't actually implicated themselves in that vision. They don't even know what that is, to be honest. They just have that as a conceptual idea that they've been too afraid to own, to put them themselves in and to experience what that might actually look like. So this is where, within your coaching, you need to clarify, you know, what does that look like? Can you tell me what it is about that goal that really excites you, that really lights you up? What does life look like for you and your family or your business partner on the other side of this? Allow them to explore the fullness and actually start creating that reality, right, before even living it. Allow them to even explore what that is. Why is such a vital goal and desire, why is this such a vital goal and desire for you to claim right now, right? Have them invest in knowing what their motivation and intent actually is. What exactly is your version of a successful freelance copyright and career? Once again, dimensionalize it, get hyper specific about what it actually means. Desire requires some form of specificity. Otherwise, it's just a vague idea. It's driving you to want to write your book because there's specific vision or moment in the future that's driving this desire. How will you know you've attained it? This is a really powerful question that I do encourage you to ask, right? Like I get and I hear that this is what you want, but how will you know it's been achieved? What does that moment look like once you realize, gosh, yes, I finally made it. So you've passed the 51% threshold of desire when you feel their statement of I want X. I want a successful career that allows me to XYZ, right? Whatever it is, if you can hear them say that with a degree of confidence, determination, and sense of inevitability, then you can be confident that you have successfully crossed into that positive threshold of healthy desire. And remember, that's what we're looking at. Just crossing over that 50%. We don't need to get someone to 100%. Pure desire without any of the longing, totally transcending any fear of not attaining it, totally transcending any unfairness they may have felt in the past, or just looking to tip the scales. All right. So next element is anticipation. And within positive anticipation, there is also typically a sense of anxiety, a sense of urgency that, gosh, this has to happen right now or else I'm screwed. And that fear of failure, if they move on, they're going to have to succeed in it, or they're going to risk all the negative shitty feelings that come with feeling like a failure. So that's what happens once desire is claimed, they'll either have a positive sense of anticipation in its pursuit and achievement, or will feel a mix of anxiety around what's going to be needed out of them, who they're going to have to grow into urgency that it needs to happen fast, and a fear of failing and that fruitlessness of the efforts of the investment of energy, attention and money that they put into its pursuit. So once again, you're looking for a feeling of lightness and excitement to sense that they have positive anticipation. So that might be something along the lines of, gosh, I'm so excited to get started pursuing this feels freaking awesome. You have no idea how long this is overdue. It's my year to really expand and grow into this direction. So these are all examples of what it might sound like for someone to be in a sense of positive anticipation. However, if you sense anxiousness, a fear of failure or urgency that this has to work right now, you'll want to use questioning and reframing to unpack it and invite them back into positive anticipation. So what might this look like? They might say, I need to make my freelance career work in the next few months as I don't have much more runway before I need to start looking for jobs again, then you might follow up. Can I ask you a question? When you look back at some of the awesome things you've already achieved, did they come from a place of pressure and urgency or relaxness and confidence? And then they might reflect and say, I'd say confidence, then you could follow up. Cool. Well, I'm confident you can do this, but I'd be even more confident if we can work together from a place of relaxness. Do you feel that's possible? So other questions for exploring the polarity of anticipation and anxiety, right? If you sense that there is a sense of urgency or anxiety coming through, you really want to probe that. You really want to use your deep questioning and your presence and possibly your reframing and all the other techniques we spoke about to allow that to be fully unpacked. So you could ask, sounds like you've been thinking about this goal for a while, haven't you? Right? And they might go on this long spiel about how it's what they've wanted. They've been afraid of taking that plunge. They've been afraid of failing. They've been afraid of what it's going to require for them. And that is going to help air out that aspect of the polarity. You can ask, if I may ask, how do you really feel about going all in on this right now? And once again, a question like this might coax out some of that fear, some of that urgency, some of that pressure that they might be feeling. You could ask, this definitely sounds like the right time. What do you now trust or see in yourself that you didn't just a few months ago? Right? So if they've been sitting on this opportunity, if they've been waiting to go all in on this for a while, and now you're hearing that like they're almost there, but there's still a bit of density and heaviness to it, you can have them affirm. What is it about them now? Right? That makes this something they could positively anticipate instead of feel anxiety around. Next element, expansion and renewed possibility of outcome. So this is also going to include elements of contraction of doubt, of hopelessness and of despair. So this is a very important note to take home, a history of struggle and failure, which most people will have when they get on a call with you will inevitably breed doubt, hopelessness and despair. In many cases, by the time someone's applying to work with you, they've already experienced some degree of this despair. So if you're sensing a disconnect between the outcome they desire and their sense of possibility around it, how possible they believe this thing actually is, you're going to want to question those feelings immediately. So you could ask on a scale of one to 10, how possible do you feel it is to move your family to write your book this year? Right? If it's less than a 10, explore that with follow up questions. You can ask when you tap into your goal of scaling your freelance business beyond 10k per month, what are the last few moments that really give you confidence and assurance that this is possible for you, right? Move them into a sense of, gosh, this might be actually more possible than I thought it was. You could ask, are you feeling energized and a sense of confidence that this will happen despite what happened last time? Remember, they might have a history of failure here. So you want to make sure that they are approaching this new opportunity with new found energy and confidence. Or you could really simply ask in the really light, easy form that could unload a whole aspect of this Gestalt. You're really hoping that this is different. You're really hoping that this time is different, aren't you? And this is where they might just kind of spill out, right? Of all the despair, of all the negative outcomes that they're filtering through, of all the doubt they still have around this outcome and give you a chance to really pierce through those layers and move them back into a state of confidence. Next element is renewed confidence, not just in the opportunity, but in themselves. And this is going to include frustration, failure, struggle, guilt, and shame. So this is tied to element three, but now this is really their relationship, not just to the opportunity, but to themselves within the opportunity. So whereas renewed responsibility is less personal and more about belief and the possibility of the outcome, renewed confidence is all about their belief in themselves. If they get into a spin around the missteps along the way, bad decisions they've made, failures, and the shame and guilt they feel around it, you know, kind of this tone like I'm useless, why can't I do this? You'll want to coach the transmutation back to renewed confidence by focusing on that invitation-based reframing we spoke about earlier in the module. So you could ask, is it possible that all those missteps, while seemingly costly, were really just part of the process that every successful person in this role has had to walk through, right? So that's an invitation-based reframe. If you had to find some value in those experiences you've had, even if they weren't fruitful in the ultimate sense, what would you find? Right? So this is reframing those negative experiences into the value they actually brought, right? The stepping stones they were. This is all about creating meaning. It's a possible that you needed to go through those steps to get super clear on what methods don't work for you. And it's a possible that with that realization, you'll save yourself a lot more time, money, and energy moving forward. I acknowledge the difficulty of those missteps, and I'm not trying to sugarcoat it, but by the sounds of it, it's made you even more fiercely committed to solving this once and for all. Would you say that's true? Right? So this is all about taking all those missteps, right? All the shame, guilt, anger, frustration around how things haven't gone their way, and finding meaning within it, right? And how it's led them to this point right now where they have possibly an even greater opportunity with greater insight, learnings, and realizations along the way that can make this time different, right? And then you could ask a simple question, what makes you confident that you're ready to do it this time around? So all of these are super powerful questions that should get them into this place of renewed confidence in themselves. And this is going to be a prerequisite for them having the confidence to say yes to your offer, and it's certainly going to be a prerequisite for them having confidence to succeed once they're in your program itself. But ultimately, you want your precline to see themselves in a positive, empowered light, giving themselves, granting themselves forgiveness and grace over those missteps and even credit for places they've condemned themselves or their efforts in the past, right? And if they can experience this in your presence, once again, that leads into resonance. Next, clarity. So clarity is also going to include elements of confusion and overwhelm. So question, do they feel clear in what's needed to overcome the issue and or achieve the desired outcome and everything it represents, right? So this is a very simple question, but you'll find that this litmus tesolone will unlock so much because if they're not clear, if they're still feeling elements of confusion and overwhelm, that's going to breed into their ability to actually take you up on your offer. So clarity is super important. If you've unpacked the past and what they've been doing, what's not been working, why it hasn't been working and help them renew a sense of possibility with your diagnosis and your prescription and renewed confidence in themselves, then clarity should be what naturally follows. It's more of an emergent of those elements. However, you do want to assess for how much clarity is present before moving into the offer. You want to check if they're clear in what they want, if they're clear on why past attempts have failed, if they're clear on what makes this possible now, and if they're clear on why they themselves can achieve it. So this is really a clarity summary of those first few elements and you want to make sure. You don't need to necessarily ask them. You just want to observe in your own presence. Do they feel clear on what they want? Do they feel clear on why the past hasn't worked out? Do they feel clear on what makes it possible now? And do they feel clear on why they can achieve it? So the feeling of clarity should feel light and filled with that positive anticipation. If you sense elements of confusion or shutdown, this tends to be stemming from a sense of overwhelm and you'll want to coach them through what elements within that aren't clear. So a question you might ask is just so we're on the same page. Are you feeling clear on what's needed to write your book this quarter? Does that resonate? So you do want to ask these deeper questions really to make sure they're clear on the core elements that are going to empower them to say yes later. Next is readiness. So a sense of readiness may include elements of remorse, regret, and resistance. So readiness is also more of a natural emergent of those other elements. If you've checked through all those other previous aspects of the Gestalt, someone should naturally be ready, right? But readiness does have its own specific polarities of remorse, regret, resistance that may need to be examined in of themselves. So readiness can include the remorse or regret of it taking so long, right? It's like, amazing, this opportunity is here, I'm feeling clear on it. But damn, like I wish I started earlier, right? Like all this damage, all this toll has happened, you know, by letting this problem fester for as long as it has, right? It may have even decayed into a more challenging state, especially if you're talking about someone's health or someone's losses in a business. So ultimately, there might be these elements of regret and remorse of not starting earlier that are getting in the way of someone feeling fully ready to explore the opportunity. So it usually will come into form of that, I wish I had taken care of this sooner. Why did I wait so long? I'm already so far behind, I've already put myself in a deeper hole, it's going to be that much harder to catch up. So these are kind of the emotions, the languaging that you want to pay attention to and want to get this might appear not as verbatim, but as subtext. And when that happens, you obviously want to unpack it. So most problems get more challenging to fix as they're ignored. And there's the dissonance and shame of feeling responsible for it, of letting that decay to encounter. So if you sense there's remorse or regret, this is your opportunity to offer reassurance of how they're empowering themselves and taking control of it now. And that some people never get there at all, right? There's still so much value and so much possibility to be proud of oneself for taking action now, no matter how far things have decayed, no matter how much greater the challenge is now versus later, they're here now, right? And that's what matters. So you could say, I'm sensing that this has been long overdue, right? So a very simple reflection like that might have them responding and unpacking the full spectrum of their remorse, right? This is long overdue and they'll go into all the reasons why, you know, they kind of regret not getting started earlier. And then you could follow up with, well, is it possible to honor yourself for being willing to do it now? You could say, well, I honor the difficulty of that path. And likewise, I invite you to honor the opportunity you have now and all you've done to get yourself in that position. If they're showing upsetness over some element of decay, once again, you can acknowledge it. Well, I acknowledge that, but let's say you weren't on this call today and you let things run their course for another three months. Can you see how much better off you are compared to that scenario? Is it possible to be appreciative and excited about the opportunity you still do have to accomplish this, right? So it's all about giving them some degree of perspective. Yes, things have decayed relative to where they could have been if people address their problems sooner, but they're still in a far better opportunity relative to where they could have been months from now if they continued to delay, right? So it's about giving them that much needed perspective so that they feel amazing and proud of themselves for taking action right now. Finally, last element is rightness. And rightness will also include wrongness in the goal they're pursuing and the path towards achieving it or the process within that path. So ideally, they'll have knowledge around your method and your process beforehand, whether that's via a guide, a webinar, your pre-call onboarding, whatever it is, and will come on the call with a degree of alignment and rightness that you can simply have them reaffirm on this call. So no one should be coming on blind with zero knowledge of your process and the way you do things. They should have a degree of resonance around that. But you do want that to be reaffirmed, right, with questioning. So what about our method resonated? How much do you know about what we do? What is it that aligns with you? The second part of the question is super important. You want them to describe their own alignment versus you kind of forcing it on them. What is it about our process that really struck you? I know you've been through a lot of programs and practitioners. What is it about our process that makes you more confident and hopeful? Are you feeling like this is finally the right path forward? Can you see yourself doing this work? So when you ask these questions without assumption, you give them the opportunity to find their own alignment, to find their own resonance, to find their own sense of rightness. And it's so much more powerful when they do it of their own volition, when they volunteer these reasons versus you pounding them throughout the call, especially around a close and the offered self of why this is for them, right? They're going to be that much more invested in things they've said and the insights they've had in the resonance that they felt and communicated to you. However, wrongness may creep in if renewed possibility and renewed confidence haven't hit that 51% threshold and they're still experiencing those elements of doubt, frustration, shame, hopelessness and despair. So if you still sense wrongness in your call, then of course you'll want to dive into the source, whether it fits about them, the outcome or you. It's really important to get clear on what that source of wrongness is. And once again, we're going to rely on our questioning, right? What I'm hearing is that you've been so committed to this change for a long time, but haven't found the right path. So this is a question you could ask for going deeper in past missteps. You could ask what I'm sensing is that you're really committed to making this work, but based on some past challenges, you're still not entirely sure of whether or not you're going to be able to do this. So this is about going deeper into their self doubt and transforming that into renewed confidence in their abilities. So remember, this isn't a script, right? This whole set of seven elements within the Gestalt, you may cover parts of this framework or all of it, but it's your presence with your pre-client that's really going to drive the conversation more than a rigid adherence to these seven elements. So these are really here as a reference of essentially what is going to come up on your calls and really the thresholds you need to cross, right? So you're not going to be putting longing, lack, pain, envy and injustice into a desire, or maybe you don't have to coach that transformation. Maybe the desire right off the bat is clear and they jump on the call and say, this is what I want and I feel amazing about it. That's the anticipation. They feel confident in themselves. They feel confident in you. They feel clarity over what to do because they've already gone through your webinar or your guide. And in these cases, right, where they're already a 51% or greater threshold on those positive elements, when they're already in a sense of rightness and readiness and clarity and the result of all this is courage and power, that's why you have calls that can last seven minutes, eight minutes, 10 minutes, right? And you're not going to want to drag that into 50 minutes and exhaust them by exploring elements of the past and the negative polarities that simply are irrelevant to that conversion or that conversation. So this is a map. Keep in mind that you may go through some of it, you may go through all of it, right? But this is here to really help you recognize what is being said, right? And how to coach people into the positive side of those polarities where it is useful, where it is necessary. So this is fluid. This is a framework that is loose on purpose because conversations are dynamic and they deserve to go in the direction that is most needed and most of service to the transformation that that specific pre-client requires. So I encourage you to put this into practice. I encourage you to re-listen to your sales calls, even once you've done before this program, right? And get into habit of identifying what part of the gestalts are actually being spoken about, how to identify what they sound like, what they look like, and get into habit, of course, of coaching people onto the positive side of the polarity. So that's it. Take a breather. Put this into practice. And I'll catch you in the next lesson. Bye for now.
